questions.question: What does ITCSS stand for?
questions.explanation: ITCSS (Inverted Triangle CSS) organizes CSS from generic (top) to explicit (bottom).

questions.question: Which ITCSS layer comes first?
questions.explanation: Settings (variables, config) is the first, most generic layer in ITCSS.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Objects layer in ITCSS?
questions.explanation: Objects are reusable layout patterns like grids, containers, and media objects.

questions.question: How do CSS cascade layers (@layer) help architecture?
questions.explanation: @layer explicitly defines the order in which CSS declarations win, preventing specificity battles.

questions.question: What is SMACSS?
options: A categorization system: base, layout, module, state, theme
questions.explanation: SMACSS (Scalable and Modular Architecture for CSS) categorizes CSS into five types.

questions.question: What is the principle of OOCSS?
options: Object-oriented CSS: separate structure from skin
questions.explanation: OOCSS separates container structure from visual skin, promoting reuse.

questions.question: What is a CSS object in architecture terms?
options: A reusable layout pattern (e.g., media object, grid)
questions.explanation: CSS objects are reusable layout patterns that don't define visual design.

questions.question: Which architecture approach uses single-purpose classes?
questions.explanation: Utility-first CSS uses single-purpose classes like .text-center, .mt-4 for rapid composition.

questions.question: What is the benefit of separating structure from skin?
options: Layout patterns can be reused with different visual themes
questions.explanation: Separating structure (layout) from skin (colors, fonts) allows layout reuse across themes.

questions.question: What defines a good CSS architecture?
options: Predictable cascade, easy maintenance, scalable organization
questions.explanation: Good CSS architecture prioritizes predictable cascade, maintainability, and scalability.

questions.question: What is a CSS component in architecture terms?
options: A self-contained UI module with its own styles
questions.explanation: A CSS component encapsulates all styles for a self-contained UI piece like a card or button.

questions.question: What layer should CSS resets go in?
questions.explanation: Resets/normalize go in the Generic layer, which sets baseline styles before elements and components.

questions.question: How does a good architecture prevent specificity wars?
options: Using flat classes and layers instead of deep nesting
questions.explanation: Flat specificity (class-based selectors) and cascade layers prevent specificity escalation.

questions.question: What is the role of utility classes in CSS architecture?
options: They provide single-purpose overrides for exception cases
questions.explanation: Utility classes are single-purpose helpers for one-off adjustments without creating new components.

questions.question: What does a CSS architecture document describe?
options: How CSS is organized, naming conventions, layer strategy, and file structure
questions.explanation: Architecture documentation explains the project's CSS organization, conventions, layers, and decision rationale.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Tools layer in ITCSS?
questions.explanation: The Tools layer contains mixins, functions, and helper classes (primarily for preprocessors).

questions.question: What is wrong with deeply nested selector in architecture?
options: They create high specificity and tight coupling to HTML structure
questions.explanation: Deep nesting creates high specificity and fragile selectors that break with HTML changes.

questions.question: What should be in the Elements layer of ITCSS?
options: Bare HTML element styles (h1, a, p, ul)
questions.explanation: The Elements layer styles bare HTML elements without classes (h1, p, a, ul, etc.).

questions.question: What is the difference between Objects and Components in ITCSS?
options: Objects are layout patterns; Components are UI modules with visual design
Objects are for mobile; Components for desktop
Objects are classes; Components are IDs
questions.explanation: Objects are reusable layout abstractions (grid, media). Components are concrete UI modules (card, button).

questions.question: When should you create a new component vs using a utility?
options: Component for reusable UI patterns, utility for one-off adjustments
questions.explanation: Create components for reusable patterns. Use utilities for one-off exceptions that don't warrant a component.
